commands.review_summary:
Review failed before ClawSweeper could summarize the requested change.

PR surface: Source +529, Tests +493, Docs +46. Total +1068 across 16 files.

Reproducibility: unclear. The review failed before ClawSweeper could establish a reproduction path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Review did not complete, so no work-lane recommendation was made.

target.title: Feat/browser screenshot vision
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: When the main model is text-only (e.g. `tencent-tokenhub/hy3-preview`), browser screenshots return image content blocks that the model cannot read — the agent sees `[image omitted]` and cannot reason about the page.
- Why it matters: Browser-use workflows are unusable with text-only main models; operators must switch to an expensive multimodal main model just to get screenshot readback.
- What changed:
  - The browser screenshot action now optionally runs the captured image through a configured vision model and returns a text description instead of an image block. This is controlled by new fields on the top-level `browser` config namespace (`models`, `visionEnabled`, `visionPrompt`, etc.). When unconfigured, behavior is unchanged.
  - Vision descriptions are wrapped with `wrapExternalContent` (same as snapshot/tabs results) to mitigate prompt injection from untrusted web page content.
  - Local screenshot file size is checked against `maxBytes` before sending to the vision provider.
  - `DescribeImageFileWithModelParams` in `src/media-understanding/runtime-types.ts` gains `profile`/`preferredProfile` fields, forwarded through `describeImageFileWithModel` to `describeImage` — enabling auth profile selection for all callers of this API, not just the browser tool.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to the agent loop, replay-history, session persistence, transport layer, or any other tool. The vision call is entirely contained within the browser extension's screenshot action. The existing `tools.media.image` config and `image_describe` tool are untouched.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related: browser-use with text-only models
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — this is a new feature, not a bug fix.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/browser/src/browser/vision.test.ts` (20 tests)
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - `isBrowserVisionEnabled` returns true/false based on config presence, `enabled: false`, empty models, missing provider/model, CLI-type entries
  - `describeBrowserImageWithVision` calls through to `describeImageFileWithModel` with correct params including prompt, timeout, profile, preferredProfile
  - Local file paths as `mediaUrl` are not forwarded (only HTTP(S) URLs)
  - HTTP(S) `mediaUrl` is correctly forwarded
  - Fallback to next candidate when primary fails
  - Empty model response treated as failure and falls through
  - All candidates failing produces aggregate error with per-candidate details
  - `maxChars` truncation with `[truncated]` marker
  - `maxBytes` enforcement via file stat before vision call
  - `profile`/`preferredProfile` forwarded to `describeImageFileWithModel`
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The helper is a pure function with injected deps — unit tests cover all branches without needing a real browser or vision API.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): `browser-tool.test.ts` mock infrastructure extended to include `describeImageFileWithModel` dep injection.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New config fields on the top-level `browser` namespace for vision model configuration:
  ```json
  {
    "browser": {
      "models": [{ "provider": "bytedance", "model": "doubao-seed-2.0-pro" }]
    }
  }
  ```
  Supported fields: `models[]` (with `provider`, `model`, `prompt`, `maxChars`, `maxBytes`, `timeoutSeconds`, `profile`, `preferredProfile`), `visionEnabled`, `visionPrompt`, `visionMaxChars`, `visionMaxBytes`, `visionTimeoutSeconds`.
- When `browser.models` contains at least one valid provider/model entry and `visionEnabled` is not `false`, every browser screenshot is automatically described by the configured vision model. The tool returns a text content block (`[analyzed by <provider>/<model>]\n<wrapped description>`) instead of an image content block. The description text is wrapped with `wrapExternalContent` to guard against prompt injection.
- When `browser.models` is **not** configured, behavior is identical to before — screenshots return image content blocks.
- Set `visionEnabled: false` to force-disable vision without removing the model list.
- If vision description fails (API error, timeout, empty response, file exceeds `maxBytes`), the tool falls back to returning the original image block plus a warning text block so the agent loop can still recover.
- `DescribeImageFileWithModelParams` now accepts optional `profile`/`preferredProfile` fields. This is a backward-compatible additive change to the media-understanding runtime contract. All existing callers are unaffected; new callers (like browser vision) can pass auth profile selection.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before (text-only main model):
  agent calls browser screenshot
    -> tool returns image content block
    -> transport replaces with "[image omitted]"
    -> agent cannot reason about page

After (browser.models configured):
  agent calls browser screenshot
    -> tool captures screenshot to disk
    -> stat file size → check maxBytes
    -> tool calls vision model (describeImageFileWithModel)
    -> wrapExternalContent(description)
    -> tool returns wrapped text description
    -> text-only main model reads description normally

After (browser.models NOT configured):
  agent calls browser screenshot
    -> tool returns image content block (unchanged)
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No — reuses existing `describeImageFileWithModel` which handles auth through the standard provider registry, SecretRef mechanism, and (now) optional auth profiles.
- New/changed network calls? Yes — when `browser.models` is configured, the browser tool makes an additional API call to the configured vision provider to describe the screenshot.
  - Risk: The screenshot image is sent to the configured vision provider's API endpoint.
  - Mitigation: This is opt-in (only when operator explicitly configures `browser.models`). The image data flow is identical to `tools.media.image` / `image_describe` tool — same `describeImageFileWithModel` pipeline, same auth, same SSRF guards.
  - Risk: Vision model descriptions contain untrusted web page content that could include prompt injection.
  - Mitigation: All vision description text is wrapped with `wrapExternalContent(text, { source: "browser", includeWarning: true })`, matching the security treatment applied to snapshot and tabs results.
  - Risk: Raw screenshot could be auto-delivered to chat channels if exposed as deliverable media (related: #44759).
  - Mitigation: Addressed — the vision-success path does not include any `MEDIA:` directive or `details.media` in the tool result. The raw screenshot file remains on disk for diagnostic purposes only but is not advertised as deliverable media, preventing auto-send of sensitive page content to chat channels.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS (darwin)
- Runtime: Node 22, OpenClaw dev build from source (npm link)
- Model/provider: Main model `tencent-tokenhub/hy3-preview` (text-only); vision model `bytedance/doubao-seed-2.0-pro`
- Integration/channel: Control UI webchat
- Relevant config:
  ```json
  {
    "browser": {
      "models": [{ "provider": "bytedance", "model": "doubao-seed-2.0-pro" }]
    }
  }
  ```

### Steps

1. Configure `browser.models` with a vision-capable provider/model
2. Start gateway, open dashboard
3. In a new session, ask the agent to take a browser screenshot (e.g. "open https://example.com and take a screenshot")

### Expected

- Agent receives a text description of the screenshot instead of an image block
- Text-only main model can read and reason about the page content
- Tool result includes `[analyzed by <provider>/<model>]` attribution
- Description text is wrapped in external content markers

### Actual

- Confirmed working: agent received text description, main model reasoned about page content normally

## Evidence

- [x] Trace/log snippets: Gateway log shows `[browser/vision] described screenshot via bytedance/doubao-seed-2.0-pro (chars=NNN)` on successful vision call
- [x] Failing before + passing after: Before config, agent sees `[image omitted]`; after config, agent reads full page description
- [x] Unit tests: 20 tests passing in `extensions/browser/src/browser/vision.test.ts`
- [x] CI: `pnpm check:changed` passes (typecheck core/extensions/tests + lint core/extensions + all guards)
- [x] Screenshot/recording

### Video: Vision model describes screenshot for text-only agent

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/32826a25-ca02-4188-9bee-cd7e4bec6639

### Video: Fallback sanitization (new, current HEAD)

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d7051b53-6935-4f8c-8899-b60fc55ce72d

Source-level evidence for the image content block that this fallback path also returns (the binary `image` content block itself is not easy to film in webchat, so it is shown via the gateway log line emitted by `imageResultFromFile` → `resolveRuntimeImageSanitization` → sharp resize):

```
2026-05-21T09:11:23.579+08:00 [browser/vision] candidate fail-on-purpose-for-fallback-demo/no-such-model failed: image description timed out after 2000ms
2026-05-21T09:11:23.614+08:00 [agents/tool-images] Image resized to fit limits: 2000x1140px 74.8KB -> 9.2KB (-87.7%)
```

Interpretation:
- Line 1 = the vision provider call rejected/timed out, so the screenshot tool entered the `catch` fallback at `extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.ts:833`.
- Line 2 = the fallback called `imageResultFromFile({ ..., imageSanitization: resolveRuntimeImageSanitization() })` with `agents.defaults.imageMaxDimensionPx: 512`, and the upstream image-result helper resized the raw 2000×1140 screenshot before the image content block was added to the tool result. Without the P2 fix, this `imageSanitization` argument was absent, so the original 2000×1140 image would have been returned unscaled.

Reproduction config used for the video:

```jsonc
{
  "agents": { "defaults": { "imageMaxDimensionPx": 512 } },
  "browser": {
    "models": [
      { "provider": "fail-on-purpose-for-fallback-demo", "model": "no-such-model", "timeoutSeconds": 2 }
    ]
  }
}
```

A non-existent vision provider id is used so the failure is deterministic and does not depend on any external network call. `visionEnabled` defaults to enabled whenever `browser.models[]` resolves at least one valid candidate, and `visionTimeoutSeconds` is only consulted as the fallback when an individual `models[].timeoutSeconds` is unset, so both top-level fields are omitted here.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Text-only main models can now read and reason about browser screenshots via a configured vision model, instead of receiving `[image omitted]`.
- Real environment tested: macOS 26.3 arm64, Node v25.8.1, OpenClaw built from source (`pnpm build` → `node dist/index.js gateway --port 18789`), main model hy3-preview (tencent-tokenhub, text-only), vision model doubao-seed-2.0-pro (bytedance), webchat dashboard.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Built source: `pnpm build`
  2. Started gateway from source: `node dist/index.js gateway --port 18789`
  3. Configured `browser.models` with `bytedance/doubao-seed-2.0-pro`
  4. Opened webchat dashboard, asked agent to screenshot a Discord page
  5. Agent captured screenshot → vision model described it → agent reasoned about page content and responded with a text summary
- Evidence after fix: Screen recording showing the full flow: agent takes screenshot, vision model processes it, agent returns a coherent text description of the page content. See "Video: After" above.
- Observed result after fix: Tool result contains `[analyzed by bytedance/doubao-seed-2.0-pro]` attribution + wrapped text description. Main model (hy3-preview) successfully reads and reasons about the Discord channel content.
- What was not tested: Remote/node browser proxy screenshots; multiple fallback candidates in sequence (only single-candidate tested live); per-agent auth profile selection.
- Fresh ClawSweeper-flagged proof at the current HEAD: scenario (b) "vision-failure fallback with `agents.defaults.imageMaxDimensionPx` configured — confirming the returned image block is downscaled by the sanitization guard" is captured by the **Video: Fallback sanitization** above plus the cited `[agents/tool-images] Image resized to fit limits: 2000x1140px 74.8KB -> 9.2KB` gateway log line (the raw `image` content block itself is not visible in webchat UI, hence the log-based supplement). Scenario (a) "directive-shaped `MEDIA:/tmp/secret.png` in page or vision output does not synthesize media delivery" is covered by the unit + integration regression tests landed in this PR (`extensions/browser/src/browser/vision.test.ts` and `extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.test.ts`).

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - Text-only main model + browser screenshot with `browser.models` configured → text description returned, agent reasons about page
  - Vision call failure → fallback to image block + warning text
  - No `browser.models` configured → original image block behavior unchanged
  - Vision text containing `MEDIA:` directive line → neutralized in tool-result text, `details.media` absent (regression test)
  - Vision failure fallback with `agents.defaults.imageMaxDimensionPx` → fallback `imageResultFromFile` receives matching `imageSanitization` (regression test)
- Edge cases checked:
  - Local file path passed as `mediaUrl` — fixed: only forward HTTP(S) URLs to describeImageFileWithModel
  - Provider mismatch (`agent-plan` vs `bytedance`) — learned that provider id must match the media-understanding registry, same as `tools.media.image`
  - `wrapExternalContent` applied to vision output — verified in code review
  - `maxBytes` file stat guard — verified via unit test
  - `profile`/`preferredProfile` forwarding — verified end-to-end through runtime-types → runtime.ts → describeImage
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Remote/node browser proxy screenshots (only tested host browser)
  - Multiple fallback candidates in sequence (only single-candidate config tested live; unit test covers this path)
  - Per-agent auth profile selection (see Known Limitations)

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

### ClawSweeper findings addressed

Previous review round:

- **[P1] Do not advertise vision screenshots as deliverable media** — Fixed: the vision-success path no longer includes any `MEDIA:` directive or `details.media` in the tool result. Raw screenshots are not auto-delivered to chat channels. Related: #44759.
- **[P2] Remove the unused schema import** — Fixed: removed orphaned `ToolsMediaUnderstandingSchema` import from `zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts`.

Latest review round:

- **[P1] Neutralize media directives in vision text** (`extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.ts:801-807`) — Fixed: introduced `neutralizeMediaDirectives` in `extensions/browser/src/browser/vision.ts`. The vision-success path now runs `described.text.trim()` through this helper *before* `wrapExternalContent`, inserting `[neutralized] ` in front of every line whose `trimStart()` begins with `MEDIA:` (case-insensitive). `wrapExternalContent` itself does not strip line-start directives and the agent media extractor (`splitMediaFromOutput`) scans every browser tool-result text block; the `browser` tool is on the trusted-media allowlist, so this guard is required to prevent untrusted page or vision-provider output (e.g. `MEDIA:/tmp/secret.png`) from synthesizing channel-deliverable media artifacts. Regression covered:
  - Unit tests in `extensions/browser/src/browser/vision.test.ts` for the helper itself (no-op fast path, mid-line `MEDIA:` is not defanged, line-start with arbitrary leading whitespace is defanged, case-insensitive, multiple directives in one blob).
  - Integration test in `extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.test.ts` (`neutralizes MEDIA: directives in vision text and does not attach media`) that drives the full `screenshot` tool execution with a vision-provider response containing `MEDIA:/tmp/secret.png` and asserts: no text-content line in the result matches `/^\s*MEDIA:/i`, the secret path text is still preserved verbatim (defanged not deleted), `details.media` is absent, and `imageResultFromFile` is never called on the success path.

- **[P2] Keep image sanitization on vision failure fallback** (`extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.ts:828-833`) — Fixed: the vision-failure catch block now passes `imageSanitization: resolveRuntimeImageSanitization()` to `imageResultFromFile`, matching what the non-vision screenshot path at the same callsite already supplies. Without this, any provider error (timeout, auth failure, oversized file) silently bypasses the existing `agents.defaults.imageMaxDimensionPx` guard added in `d5cc0d53b7e3`. Regression covered:
  - Integration test in `extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.test.ts` (`preserves screenshot image sanitization on vision failure fallback`) that configures `agents.defaults.imageMaxDimensionPx: 1600`, forces `describeImageFileWithModel` to reject, and asserts the fallback `imageResultFromFile` call receives `imageSanitization: { maxDimensionPx: 1600 }` along with the failure `extraText`.



## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes — no behavior change without explicit `browser.models` config.
- Config/env changes? Yes — new optional fields on the top-level `browser` config namespace (`models`, `visionEnabled`, `visionPrompt`, `visionMaxChars`, `visionMaxBytes`, `visionTimeoutSeconds`).
- Migration needed? No — purely additive.
- `DescribeImageFileWithModelParams` gains two optional fields (`profile`, `preferredProfile`) — backward compatible, no existing callers affected.

## Known Limitations

- **scope/attachments fields not implemented**: The `tools.media.image` config shape includes `scope` and `attachments` fields, but these are not exposed for `browser` because the browser tool's vision is always triggered on the just-captured screenshot — there is no attachment selection or scope gating semantic. These can be added in a follow-up if a use case emerges.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Vision API latency adds to screenshot tool response time (typically 5–25s depending on provider).
  - Mitigation: Configurable `timeoutSeconds`; on failure, falls back to returning the raw image block so the agent loop is never blocked.
- Risk: Operator configures wrong provider id (e.g. `agent-plan` instead of `bytedance`) and gets silent failures.
  - Mitigation: Error message surfaces the provider/model that failed; the config shape mirrors `tools.media.image` so operators can copy working config directly.
- Risk: Oversized screenshots sent to vision providers.
  - Mitigation: File size checked against `maxBytes` (default 10 MB) before vision call; oversized files skip the candidate with a logged warning.
- Risk: Web page content in vision descriptions could contain prompt injection.
  - Mitigation: Vision output wrapped with `wrapExternalContent` with warning, matching existing snapshot/tabs treatment.

commands.command: proof: override the circuit breaker implementation has been verified with unit tests covering: 1. breaker opens after 2 consecutive generic fallbacks 2. remaining splits and merge are skipped (call-count proof) 3. partialsummaryfallback correctly classified as non-generic (p2 fix applied in commit ca7afac1c) real compaction runtime proof requires pnpm build which is not available in current codex worktree. request maintainer override to allow ci to verify in real linux node 24 environment.
commands.freeform_prompt: proof: override The circuit breaker implementation has been verified with unit tests covering: 1. Breaker opens after 2 consecutive generic fallbacks 2. Remaining splits and merge are skipped (call-count proof) 3. partialSummaryFallback correctly classified as non-generic (P2 fix applied in commit ca7afac1c) Real compaction runtime proof requires pnpm build which is not available in current Codex worktree. Request maintainer override to allow CI to verify in real Linux Node 24 environment.

target.title: fix(compaction): add circuit breaker to stop token burn when summarizer unavailable
target.body:
## Summary

When the summarizer model is persistently unavailable (rate-limited, overloaded, misconfigured), `summarizeInStages` calls `summarizeWithFallback` for every split, each burning 3+ retries per call. With N splits, this burns N x 3 x chunkTokens before finally throwing.

Add a circuit breaker in the `summarizeInStages` split loop:

- Track consecutive generic fallback results via typed `FallbackSummaryResult.isGenericFallback`
- After 2 consecutive fallback splits, stop the loop
- Return partial summaries collected so far
- Reset counter on successful split response

Also fix TS errors and mock issues in test files:
- `compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts`: testModel type bypass
- `dreaming.test.ts`: getSession mock

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #58838
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

Token burn during persistent summarizer outages causes wasted API calls and slow failure recovery. Circuit breaker stops repeated fallback attempts after 2 consecutive generic failures, preserving partial summaries with useful context.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Circuit breaker prevents token burn when summarizer is persistently unavailable. After 2 consecutive generic fallback splits, the loop stops. Partial summaries that contain successful model output are not counted as generic fallbacks.

Real environment tested: Vitest test run on Linux Node 22 (local checkout).

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts

Evidence after fix:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts

✓ compaction circuit breaker > opens after 2 consecutive generic fallbacks
✓ compaction circuit breaker > skips remaining splits and merge retry  
✓ compaction circuit breaker > happy path completes all splits

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       3 passed (3)
```

Fix applied: partialSummaryFallback now returns isGenericFallback=false (contains useful progress from earlier chunks), only truly generic fallback (no partial summary) returns isGenericFallback=true.

Observed result after fix: Partial summaries preserve useful session context while still stopping repeated calls on persistent generic failures.

What was not tested: Live compaction with real unavailable summarizer model. Unable to provide real unavailable-summarizer proof due to local environment complexity. Requesting maintainer proof override.

## Changes

- `src/agents/compaction.ts`: FallbackSummaryResult type, circuit breaker in summarizeInStages, partialSummaryFallback classification fix
- `src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts`: 3 regression tests + testModel type fix
- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts`: getSession mock

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — design issue, not runtime bug.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

Unit tests cover:
1. Breaker opens after 2 consecutive generic fallbacks
2. Remaining splits and merge retry are skipped (call-count proof)
3. Happy path completes all splits without breaker

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Sessions may retain less compacted context during sustained summarizer outages (breaker stops after 2 generic failures). Partial summaries from successful splits are preserved.

## Security Impact (required)

- [ ] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [ ] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [ ] New/changed network calls? No
- [ ] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [ ] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Circuit breaker behavior (unit tests)
- Edge cases checked: partialSummaryFallback classification (contains earlier successful chunks)
- What you did NOT verify: Live unavailable-summarizer compaction run

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes
- [ ] Config/env changes? No
- [ ] Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

Session context may be truncated during sustained summarizer outages. Circuit breaker threshold (2) chosen to balance token savings vs context retention.

Fixes #58838
target.author: tanshanshan

commands.reason: author association CONTRIBUTOR and repository permission read are not allowed

target.title: fix(compaction): add circuit breaker to stop token burn when summarizer unavailable
target.body:
## Summary

When the summarizer model is persistently unavailable (rate-limited, overloaded, misconfigured), `summarizeInStages` calls `summarizeWithFallback` for every split, each burning 3+ retries per call. With N splits, this burns N x 3 x chunkTokens before finally throwing.

Add a circuit breaker in the `summarizeInStages` split loop:

- Track consecutive generic fallback results via typed `FallbackSummaryResult.isGenericFallback`
- After 2 consecutive fallback splits, stop the loop
- Return partial summaries collected so far
- Reset counter on successful split response

Also fix TS errors and mock issues in test files:
- `compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts`: testModel type bypass
- `dreaming.test.ts`: getSession mock

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #58838
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

Token burn during persistent summarizer outages causes wasted API calls and slow failure recovery. Circuit breaker stops repeated fallback attempts after 2 consecutive generic failures, preserving partial summaries with useful context.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Circuit breaker prevents token burn when summarizer is persistently unavailable. After 2 consecutive generic fallback splits, the loop stops. Partial summaries that contain successful model output are not counted as generic fallbacks.

Real environment tested: Vitest test run on Linux Node 22 (local checkout).

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts

Evidence after fix:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts

✓ compaction circuit breaker > opens after 2 consecutive generic fallbacks
✓ compaction circuit breaker > skips remaining splits and merge retry  
✓ compaction circuit breaker > happy path completes all splits

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       3 passed (3)
```

Fix applied: partialSummaryFallback now returns isGenericFallback=false (contains useful progress from earlier chunks), only truly generic fallback (no partial summary) returns isGenericFallback=true.

Observed result after fix: Partial summaries preserve useful session context while still stopping repeated calls on persistent generic failures.

What was not tested: Live compaction with real unavailable summarizer model. Unable to provide real unavailable-summarizer proof due to local environment complexity. Requesting maintainer proof override.

## Changes

- `src/agents/compaction.ts`: FallbackSummaryResult type, circuit breaker in summarizeInStages, partialSummaryFallback classification fix
- `src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts`: 3 regression tests + testModel type fix
- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts`: getSession mock

## Root Cause (if applicable)

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

Unit tests cover:
1. Breaker opens after 2 consecutive generic fallbacks
2. Remaining splits and merge retry are skipped (call-count proof)
3. Happy path completes all splits without breaker

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Sessions may retain less compacted context during sustained summarizer outages (breaker stops after 2 generic failures). Partial summaries from successful splits are preserved.

## Security Impact (required)

- [ ] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [ ] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [ ] New/changed network calls? No
- [ ] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [ ] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Circuit breaker behavior (unit tests)
- Edge cases checked: partialSummaryFallback classification (contains earlier successful chunks)
- What you did NOT verify: Live unavailable-summarizer compaction run

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes
- [ ] Config/env changes? No
- [ ] Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

Session context may be truncated during sustained summarizer outages. Circuit breaker threshold (2) chosen to balance token savings vs context retention.

Fixes #58838

commands.author: tanshanshan

target.title: fix(compaction): add circuit breaker to stop token burn when summarizer unavailable
target.body:
## Summary

When the summarizer model is persistently unavailable (rate-limited, overloaded, misconfigured), `summarizeInStages` calls `summarizeWithFallback` for every split, each burning 3+ retries per call. With N splits, this burns N x 3 x chunkTokens before finally throwing.

Add a circuit breaker in the `summarizeInStages` split loop:

- Track consecutive generic fallback results via typed `FallbackSummaryResult.isGenericFallback`
- After 2 consecutive fallback splits, stop the loop
- Return partial summaries collected so far
- Reset counter on successful split response

Also fix TS errors and mock issues in test files:
- `compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts`: testModel type bypass
- `dreaming.test.ts`: getSession mock

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #58838
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

Token burn during persistent summarizer outages causes wasted API calls and slow failure recovery. Circuit breaker stops repeated fallback attempts after 2 consecutive generic failures, preserving partial summaries with useful context.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Circuit breaker prevents token burn when summarizer is persistently unavailable. After 2 consecutive generic fallback splits, the loop stops. Partial summaries that contain successful model output are not counted as generic fallbacks.

Real environment tested: Vitest test run on Linux Node 22 (local checkout).

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts

Evidence after fix:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts

✓ compaction circuit breaker > opens after 2 consecutive generic fallbacks
✓ compaction circuit breaker > skips remaining splits and merge retry  
✓ compaction circuit breaker > happy path completes all splits

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       3 passed (3)
```

Fix applied: partialSummaryFallback now returns isGenericFallback=false (contains useful progress from earlier chunks), only truly generic fallback (no partial summary) returns isGenericFallback=true.

Observed result after fix: Partial summaries preserve useful session context while still stopping repeated calls on persistent generic failures.

What was not tested: Live compaction with real unavailable summarizer model. Unable to provide real unavailable-summarizer proof due to local environment complexity. Requesting maintainer proof override.

## Changes

- `src/agents/compaction.ts`: FallbackSummaryResult type, circuit breaker in summarizeInStages, partialSummaryFallback classification fix
- `src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts`: 3 regression tests + testModel type fix
- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts`: getSession mock

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — design issue, not runtime bug.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

Unit tests cover:
1. Breaker opens after 2 consecutive generic fallbacks
2. Remaining splits and merge retry are skipped (call-count proof)
3. Happy path completes all splits without breaker

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Sessions may retain less compacted context during sustained summarizer outages (breaker stops after 2 generic failures). Partial summaries from successful splits are preserved.

## Security Impact (required)

- [ ] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [ ] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [ ] New/changed network calls? No
- [ ] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [ ] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Circuit breaker behavior (unit tests)
- Edge cases checked: partialSummaryFallback classification (contains earlier successful chunks)
- What you did NOT verify: Live unavailable-summarizer compaction run

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes
- [ ] Config/env changes? No
- [ ] Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

Session context may be truncated during sustained summarizer outages. Circuit breaker threshold (2) chosen to balance token savings vs context retention.

Fixes #58838
